content:
🔥 ffmpeg-over-ip | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling media servers (like Jellyfin or Emby) or other applications to utilize powerful remote GPU transcoding resources without complex infrastructure changes.
✨ Key Features: • GPU-accelerated FFmpeg transcoding from anywhere • No GPU passthrough or shared filesystems required • Client/server architecture over a single TCP port
📖 Summary: ffmpeg-over-ip is a Go-based solution that allows applications to leverage GPU-accelerated FFmpeg transcoding on remote servers without requiring direct GPU access, complex setups like PCIe passthrough, or shared filesystems. It acts as a proxy, forwarding FFmpeg commands and file I/O over a TCP connection to a server running a patched FFmpeg instance.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
